name: WTC 500 RO System
description: WTC 500 Reverse Osmosis System is a modular water purification system which produces drinking water at a rate of 550 L/hr from natural water sources.

taxonomy.sub_category: WTC 500 RO System Updated on January 17, 2024 · Created on September 27, 2018 WTC 500 RO System WTC 500 Reverse Osmosis System is a modular water purification system which produces drinking water at a rate of 550 L/hr from natural water sources. Developed By Kärcher Futuretech Tested By WTC 500 RO System Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description WTC 500 RO water purification system pumps water from a natural source and uses a reverse osmosis process. The system operates on a multi-barrier principle to remove pollutants from water. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being Target Users (Target Impact Group) Community, NGOs Distributors / Implementing Organizations None Countries Germany, Luxembourg, Russia, Senegal, Singapore, United Arab Emirates, United Kingdom, United States Manufacturing/Building Method The equipment and systems are primarily produced at the Karcher factory in Obersontheim, Germany. Intellectural Property Type Trademark User Provision Model Organizations can obtain the product directly from the manufacturer through the website or international trade shows and exhibitions they participate in. Karcher Futuretech also organizes workshops and congresses for their customers. Distributions to Date Status Unknown Description of the combined methods Reverse osmosis, physical filtration Manufacturer-specified water treatment rate (L/hr) 500 L/hr Bacteria reduction Unknown Virus reduction Unknown Protozoa reduction Unknown Heavy metals and/or arsenic reduction Unknown Maximum recommended influent turbidity level (NTU) Unknown Effluent turbidity levels (NTU) Unknown Safe water storage capacity (L) None Manufacturer-specified lifetime volume (L) 13,200 L Consumables Unknown Design Specifications The WTC 500 uses reverse osmosis process with multiple barriers to remove pollutants. Water is pumped through the system's single-stage (single pass) or two-stage (double pass for highly contaminated raw waters) filtration. The double pass system is optional and is necessary only for highly contaminated water conditions like after attack or industrial catastrophes. Technical Support Karcher Futuretech provides instructions and training at the time of installment. The manufacturer also provide aftersales services. Contact details are available on the website. Replacement Components Contact the service team for replacements and spare parts. Lifecycle Unknown Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters The manufacturers of WTC RO 500 claim to "remove all pollution from the raw water that is invisible to the naked eye, including salts dissolved in the water" Vetted Performance Status Karcher Futuretech is certified in quality and environmental management by ISO, REACH and AQAP 2110. The company also has its own quality policy. Safety The instrument should be handled only after training or under trained supervision. Complementary Technical Systems Water storage devices Academic Research and References “Reverse Osmosis & Commercial Water Treatment Systems,” Pureaqua.com.
